SEARCH
Package com.gcu.business

Class UserDatabaseService

java.lang.Object
com.gcu.business.UserDatabaseService
@Service public class UserDatabaseService extends Object
Service class for handling user-related database operations. This class interacts with the UserRepository to perform CRUD operations on user data. It includes methods for adding a new user, finding a user by username, checking if a username already exists, and validating user login credentials. Each method includes logging for better traceability and debugging.

  • Constructor Summary

    Description
    UserDatabaseService(UserRepository userRepository)
    Constructor for UserDatabaseService.

  • Constructor Details

    • UserDatabaseService

      public UserDatabaseService(UserRepository userRepository)
      Constructor for UserDatabaseService. It takes a UserRepository as a parameter and initializes the userRepository field.
      Parameters:
      userRepository - The UserRepository instance to be used for database operations related to users. This is typically injected by Spring's dependency injection mechanism.

  • Method Details

    • addUser

      public UserEntity addUser(UserEntity user)
      Adds a new user to the database. It takes a UserEntity object as a parameter and saves it using the userRepository.
      Parameters:
      user - The UserEntity object representing the user to be added to the database. The object should contain the username and password of the user.
      Returns:
      The saved UserEntity object with the generated ID.

    • findByUsername

      public UserEntity findByUsername(String username)
      Finds a user by their username. It takes a username as a parameter and returns the corresponding UserEntity object if found, or null if not found.
      Parameters:
      username - The username of the user to find.
      Returns:
      The UserEntity object corresponding to the given username, or null if no user is found.

    • usernameExists

      public boolean usernameExists(String username)
      Checks if a username already exists in the database. It takes a username as a parameter and returns true if the username exists, or false if it does not.
      Parameters:
      username - The username to check for existence in the database.
      Returns:
      true if the username exists in the database, false otherwise.

    • validateUser

      public UserEntity validateUser(String username, String password)
      Validates a user's login credentials. It takes a username and password as parameters and returns the corresponding UserEntity object if the credentials are valid, or null if they are invalid.
      Parameters:
      username - The username of the user to validate.
      password - The password of the user to validate.
      Returns:
      The UserEntity object corresponding to the given username if the credentials are valid, or null if the credentials are invalid.
